what_went_well: Most HITL infrastructure was already implemented: foundry-common.sh, foundry-run.sh, foundry-telegram.sh, foundry.sh commands, telegram-qa bot, monitor QA view, tasks.ts HITL fields, handoff-template.md, CONTEXT-CONTRACT.md
Core agents (u-architect, u-coder, u-auditor, u-validator, u-tester, u-documenter, u-investigator, u-merger) already had HITL protocol
User-facing EN docs already existed at docs/pipeline/en/human-in-the-loop.md
Added HITL protocol to all remaining agents: u-deployer, u-doctor, u-ops-agent, u-planner, u-reviewer, u-security-review, u-translater
Added full HITL protocol to u-summarizer (had partial qa.json reference but missing protocol section)
Created UA translation of human-in-the-loop docs at docs/pipeline/ua/human-in-the-loop.md
what_went_wrong: No tasks.md was provided — had to derive implementation scope from the OpenSpec document directly
The spec's Section 10 file list was the primary guide for what needed to be done
improvement_suggestions: Future tasks should include a tasks.md with explicit checkboxes to avoid scope ambiguity
The spec mentions agent-to-agent Q&A escalation (Section 14) but the foundry-run.sh implementation doesn't include the u-architect Q&A responder mode — this could be a follow-up
Spec section 13 (Wait Timeout Strategy) timeout enforcement logic is not yet implemented in the orchestrator — only the Telegram notification functions exist
deviations_from_spec: The spec mentions monitor/src/components/QAView.tsx as a separate file, but the QAView component is embedded in App.tsx — this is functionally equivalent and the existing implementation is complete

Agent-to-agent Q&A escalation (Section 14) is documented in the spec but the orchestrator implementation in foundry-run.sh does not include the u-architect Q&A responder mode call — this is a pre-existing gap, not introduced by this task
